titleStmt.title: Letter from William J. Thompson to the Under Secretary, 31 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William J. Thompson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir William John Thompson (1861 - 1929) M.D., concerning the alleged false registration of the death of Mrs. Mary (Gordon) Meehan in 1898; apparently the death was correctly registered in 1909. The reply from the Under Secretary’s office, to the effect that the matter is in the hands of the police, is also available here.Sir William John Thompson, physician, served as Registrar General for Ireland from 1909 to 1926. He was knighted for his contribution to medicine in 1907.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5618/5713/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
General Register Office, CHARLEMONT HOUSE, DUBLIN, 31st March, 1916. Sir, With reference to my letter of the 24th January last, with
enclosures relative to the alleged false registration of the
death of Mrs. Mary (Gordon) Meehan in the year 1898, may I re-
quest you will be so good as to say whether any action has been
taken in the matter. The death in question was apparently correctly registered in 1908,
and the information is required so that the entries in the
Death Register of Luranboy District may be dealt with in
accordance with the decision arrived at. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, William J Thompson
Registrar General. The Under Secretary, &c., &c. Dublin Castle. Reply that the papers are
in the hands of the Police
for inquiring and report as
the evidence available to
sustain a prosecution.
1.4.16 Done
P.F 3/4. 3/4
No. 5713. 3rd April, 1916. Sir, In reply to your letter of the 31st ultimo
regarding the alleged false registration of the death
of Mrs. May (Gordon) Meehan in the year 1898, I
am directed by the Lords Justices to inform you that
the papers are in the hands of the Police for
inquiry and report as to the evidence available to
sustain a prosecution. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant,
WPJ Connolly This is signed. Located on the bottom left of the document. The Registrar General, Charlemont House, Dublin.
